logistics.drive_notes: I-70 westbound, chain law applies, brutal weekend traffic
logistics.lodging_notes: Silverthorne/Dillon have budget lodging; on-mountain is pricey

metadata.reputation: Highest base in CO, legendary late-season into June, no-frills soul mountain
metadata.terrain_character: High alpine bowls, steep chutes, exposed ridgelines; not for beginners
metadata.snow_quality_notes: High elevation = consistently cold dry powder; wind can scour exposed terrain
metadata.crowd_factor: Low midweek, moderate weekends; locals mountain
metadata.logistics_notes: US-6 over Loveland Pass — road can close in storms; no lodging at mountain

metadata.reputation: Iconic Colorado destination, massive above-treeline bowl terrain
metadata.terrain_character: Wide open bowls above treeline plus groomed cruisers; can be wind-scoured up high
metadata.snow_quality_notes: High elevation keeps snow cold; lower mountain can suffer in warm spells
metadata.crowd_factor: High on weekends and holidays; moderate midweek
metadata.logistics_notes: Hwy 9 from I-70 at Frisco; free town shuttles; avoid I-70 eastbound Sunday PM

logistics.drive_notes: Little Cottonwood Canyon road can close for avalanche control; check UDOT before leaving
logistics.lodging_notes: Salt Lake City base is economical; Snowbird/Alta on-mountain lodging is expensive but unbeatable

metadata.reputation: Skiers-only Wasatch legend; some of the deepest, driest snow in North America
metadata.terrain_character: All-mountain with world-class steeps; the Rustler and High Rustler chutes are classics
metadata.snow_quality_notes: Persistent low-density Utah powder; Albion bowl holds snow long after storms
metadata.crowd_factor: Manageable; skiers-only keeps snowboarders out, improves lift lines
metadata.logistics_notes: LCC road can close for avalanche control — leave early or stay on-mountain

metadata.reputation: Big vert, big terrain, big storm totals; one of the top resorts in North America
metadata.terrain_character: Steep and challenging; massive cirque with cliffs and chutes; Mineral Basin adds mellow terrain
metadata.snow_quality_notes: Same dry Utah powder as Alta; tram to Hidden Peak opens up the whole mountain
metadata.crowd_factor: Busy on storm days; tram line can be long; Mineral Basin is quieter
metadata.logistics_notes: Combined Alta-Snowbird ticket available; LCC road closure risk same as Alta
